questions.prompt: I delay starting important tasks even when I know exactly what to do.
I underestimate how long tasks will take.
When a task feels large, I freeze instead of taking the first step.
If feedback feels critical, my productivity drops quickly.
Noise, clutter, or notifications easily derail my work.
After interruptions, it takes me a long time to get back on track.
I start tasks before clarifying milestones or completion criteria.
I begin strong but lose momentum on multi-day work.
I rely on deadline pressure to finally begin.
My schedule often assumes ideal conditions instead of realistic constraints.
When priorities shift, I feel overloaded and struggle to re-sequence tasks.
If I miss one day, it is hard to recover momentum the next day.
